name: Chimera
description: The Chimera is a monstrous fire-breathing hybrid creature of Greek mythology. It is typically depicted as having the head of a lion, the body of a goat, and the tail of a serpent. Some depictions include a goat's head rising from the middle of its back. The Chimera was a fearsome beast, known for its destructive nature and terrifying appearance. It was said to be incredibly strong and agile, making it a formidable opponent. The Chimera was ultimately slain by the hero Bellerophon, who rode the winged horse Pegasus and used a lead-tipped spear to defeat the monster. The heat of the Chimera's breath melted the lead, which then flowed down its throat and suffocated it. Its legend persists as a symbol of the monstrous and the impossible, a testament to the power of imagination and the challenges faced by heroes of old. The Chimera's fearsome reputation and unique combination of animal parts continue to fascinate and inspire artists and storytellers to this day, solidifying its place as one of the most iconic creatures in Greek mythology. The creature is a symbol of chaos and the unnatural, embodying the fears and anxieties of the ancient Greeks.
subtitle: Fire-breathing hybrid monster
appearance: The Chimera is a grotesque combination of several animals. It has the head and foreparts of a lion, the body of a goat, and a serpent as its tail. Some accounts also describe it as having a goat's head sprouting from its back. Its fur is typically described as tawny or reddish-brown, with scales on its serpent tail. The creature is often depicted as being quite large and imposing, capable of inspiring terror with its mere presence.
habitat: The Chimera is said to have roamed the region of Lycia in Asia Minor, terrorizing the local population and destroying crops. It preferred mountainous and desolate areas, where it could easily ambush its prey. Its lair was often described as being a dark and smoky cave.
behavior: The Chimera was a savage and destructive creature, driven by a primal instinct to hunt and kill. It was known for its unpredictable and aggressive behavior, attacking anything that crossed its path. Its fire breath made it an especially dangerous predator, capable of incinerating its victims from a distance. The Chimera was a solitary creature, rarely interacting with others of its kind (if any existed).

keyMyths.title: Bellerophon and the Chimera
keyMyths.description: Bellerophon, riding Pegasus, was tasked with slaying the Chimera. He succeeded by using a lead-tipped spear, which melted in the Chimera's throat and suffocated it.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: One of the earliest and most important sources describing the Chimera.
sources.text: One of the earliest and most important sources describing the Chimera.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Provides a genealogical account of the Chimera's parentage.

sources.description: Offers a more detailed account of Bellerophon's encounter with the Chimera.
